seo.title: Able Rehab — Neurorehabilitation with the GripAble Sensor | Able Care
seo.description: Able Rehab uses the GripAble sensor to train the four core movements of the hand and wrist with engaging activities, goal tracking, and clinically validated grip tests.

blocks.headline: Able Rehab
blocks.subtitle: Utilizing the GripAble sensor for neurorehabilitation. Train the hand and wrist, motivate patients with engaging activities, and track progress with objective data.
blocks.backgroundImageAlt: Therapist guiding a patient through a GripAble rehabilitation session

blocks.heading: Train the four core movements of the hand and wrist

metrics.name: Grip / Release
metrics.description: Opening and closing the hand against resistance. Rebuilds the fundamental power grip needed for everyday tasks like holding a cup, turning a key, or shaking hands.

metrics.name: Pronation / Supination
metrics.description: Rotating the forearm palm-up and palm-down. Restores the control needed to turn a doorknob, accept change, or pour from a jug.

metrics.name: Wrist Radial / Ulnar Deviation
metrics.description: Tilting the wrist side to side toward the thumb and little finger. Trains the lateral control required for writing, brushing teeth, and using utensils.

metrics.name: Wrist Extension / Flexion
metrics.description: Bending the wrist up and down. Recovers the range of motion that underpins typing, lifting, and almost every functional reach.

blocks.headline: Engaging activities that keep patients training
blocks.description: Therapy is only effective if patients keep doing it. Able Rehab turns repetitions into gameplay, so the work that drives recovery feels like something patients want to come back to.

bulletPoints.text: Fun, motivating activities that meet clinical goals
Multiple difficulty levels to grade each patient
Assess each individual's ability, then set the activities to match — and adjust as they progress

blocks.imageAlt: Circus Escape — an engaging Able Rehab activity that trains hand and wrist movements

blocks.headline: An activity for every movement
blocks.description: Different activities target different movements. Pufferfish trains wrist flexion; other games train grip, pronation, supination, and ulnar/radial deviation — so clinicians can match the activity to each patient's clinical goal.
blocks.imageAlt: Pufferfish — an Able Rehab activity that trains wrist flexion

blocks.headline: Goal setting and achievement tracking
blocks.description: Objective progress data motivates patients and gives clinicians the evidence they need for payers, families, and care teams.

bulletPoints.text: Set goals by minutes of training or repetitions completed
Generate objective progress reports to motivate patients and report to payers
Monitor patient activity remotely between sessions

blocks.imageAlt: Able Rehab Achievements screen showing today's repetitions broken down by movement against goals

blocks.headline: Hand function tests
blocks.description: Clinically validated grip strength testing built into the same workflow as therapy — so assessment, training, and reporting all live in one place.

bulletPoints.text: Clinically proven grip strength test with published clinical norms
Test grip in multiple ways: single maximum grip, rapid exchange, and endurance
Easy, stress-free recalibration in under a minute

blocks.imageAlt: Able Rehab Single Maximum Grip Test screen showing left hand strength compared to right

blocks.heading: See Able Rehab in your clinic.
blocks.body: 20 minutes with a clinician who has used Able Rehab in practice — book a walkthrough today.
